name: Functional Bulk Dynamic
description: Multi-week hybrid program for getting functionally large. 3 heavy gym days (Push/Pull/Legs) for size and strength, 2 kettlebell days for conditioning, plus an optional Zone 2 day. Unlike the standard Functional Bulk, this version uses fixed exercises each week (no randomization) so you can track progression, and evolves week-to-week with different exercises and rep schemes.
creator: Custom

weeks.label: Foundation
weeks.description: Establish your baseline. Learn the movement patterns, find your working weights, and build the habit. All exercises are fixed so you know exactly what you're doing each session.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: Core, mobility, bodyweight legs. ~30 min on a mat.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: Hip-heavy mobility + deep stretches. Everything is sore — move through it.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 15 min hip primer. No leg work — just open up for squats.

tips: Focus on learning the movement patterns — form over weight
Find your working weights: the last 2 reps of each set should be challenging but doable
Write down your weights so you can beat them next week
If you can complete all sets at the top of the rep range, increase weight next session

weeks.label: Accumulate
weeks.description: You know your working weights. Now beat them. Same core lifts with small accessory swaps to keep it fresh. Your body is adapting — you should feel less wrecked than Week 1.

schedule.label: PUSH

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min shoulder/chest opener. Band pull-aparts, arm circles, easy push-ups.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s + pull-ups 2-3x5 (easy, don't go to failure). Pull-ups don't interfere with push day.

schedule.label: KB FULL BODY

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: Same flow as W1 — core, mobility, bodyweight legs. ~30 min on a mat.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s + pull-ups 2-3x5. Tomorrow is Pull day — keep these easy.

schedule.label: PULL

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min back/lat primer. Dead hangs, scap pulls, band pull-aparts.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s only. Skip home pull-ups — you're doing them at the gym today.

schedule.label: KB CONDITIONING

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: Same hip-heavy flow as W1. You're sore but less sore than last week. Move through it.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s only. Back recovering from Pull day yesterday.

schedule.label: LEGS

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: Same 15 min hip primer. Don't skip it this week — your squats will thank you.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s + pull-ups 2-3x5. Legs and pull-ups don't compete.

schedule.label: ZONE 2 / OPTIONAL
schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s + pull-ups 2-3x5 if you feel good. Easy day.

schedule.label: REST
schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s. That's it — rest day.

tips: You know your working weights — hit them from set 1, no ramping up
Goal: add 1-2 reps per exercise compared to W1, or add 5lbs where you topped the rep range
New exercises this week: pec deck, overhead tricep extension, DB rows, step-ups, KB high pulls, Turkish get-ups
Turkish get-ups: go LIGHT. This is a skill you're learning, not a strength test.
Try to do the morning flows this week — W1 you skipped most of them

weeks.label: Intensify
weeks.description: You know your body now. Weights go up where W2 was too easy (leg curls, calf raises, cable lat raises). New movements: push press on push day, preacher curl on pull day, bent press + cossack squat on KB day. Tuesday Explore / Thursday Refine is locked in.

schedule.label: PUSH

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: NEW — 12 min warrior flow + scorpion stretch + hollow body. More intense than W1-W2.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s + pull-ups 2-3x5-8 (push for 8 this week). Pull-ups don't interfere with push day.

schedule.label: KB FULL BODY (EXPLORE)

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: NEW — 15 min harder core (hollow rocks, plank shoulder taps) + KB-specific hip/thoracic mobility for bent press and cossack squat.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s + pull-ups 2-3x5. Tomorrow is Pull day — keep these easy.

schedule.label: PULL

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: NEW — 12 min back primer + deeper hip openers (frog stretch, happy baby). Upgraded from W2.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s only. Skip home pull-ups — you're doing them at the gym today.

schedule.label: KB CONDITIONING (REFINE)

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: NEW — 15 min recovery-focused. Plow pose, pigeon, frog stretch, light core. If time-crunched, skip core block.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s only. Back recovering from Pull day yesterday.

schedule.label: LEGS

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: NEW — 15 min dynamic squat prep. Warrior flow, skandasana, crow pose attempt. Primes heavy squats.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s + pull-ups 2-3x5-8. Legs and pull-ups don't compete.

schedule.label: ZONE 2 / OPTIONAL
schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s + pull-ups 2-3x5-8 if you feel good. Easy day.

options.label: GTC Floor Workout
options.notes: Boredom-busting floor workout: barre burn, plyo sweat, push-up party, core destroyer. ~25 min, no equipment. Do all 4 blocks or cherry-pick.

activity.name: Bay Area Hike
activity.notes: Get outside. Conversational pace. Last week's hikes were perfect Zone 2.

schedule.label: REST
schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s. That's it — rest day.

tips: Weights go up this week where W2 was too easy: leg curls (90), calf raises (450), cable lat raises (20)
Push press replaces strict shoulder press — use leg drive to go heavier overhead
Preacher curl replaces hammer curl — way harder, note which EZ bar weight is your working weight
KB Explore day: bent press and cossack squat are new. Go light, learn the patterns.
KB Refine day: drill Tuesday's new stuff + heavier Simba lift and overhead lunges
WRITE DOWN which lat pulldown and cable row machine you use at Arrillaga
DB rows are now a planned pyramid — stop pretending it was accidental

weeks.label: GTC Week
weeks.description: GTC week — schedule shifted mid-week. Wed became a rest day (9am-7pm demos). Pull moved to Thu, Legs Fri, KB Sat optional. Fresh legs for squats again — no KB the day before.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: NEW — 12 min. Thread-the-needle + wall angels + puppy pose. Different from W3.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — thoracic + hip flow. Thread-the-needle + cossack bodyweight.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: NEW — 12 min. Scapular pull-ups + dead hang + supine twists. Back primer.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: NEW — 15 min. Pigeon pose + glute bridge. Legs are FRESH — rest day yesterday, no KB day before.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — same flow as Tuesday. Light and easy end-of-week.

tips: GTC week: schedule shifted — Wed rest, Thu pull, Fri legs, Sat KB optional.
Bench: no warmup sets at 55 — 65/side is your working weight now. Start there.
Legs on Friday with FRESH legs — no KB the day before for the first time. Use that.
KB Explore Tuesday: bent press 30lb, windmill 25lb.
KB Refine Saturday: finisher is 2 rounds only — legs day was yesterday.
DB row top set: 75lb PR attempt. Brace hard.

weeks.label: Shuffle Week
weeks.description: Same exercises, reordered to get honest numbers. Push press moves to station 1 (before chest fatigue) — does 50/side go overhead with fresh shoulders? Preacher curl moves right after pull-ups (before cable curls kill biceps). Legs stay Friday. Thursday KB Refine is genuinely light — skill drilling only, no leg loading. If meetings push Thursday late, swap Thu KB / Fri Legs freely.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 12 min — overhead mobility priority. Pike push-ups new. Push press is FIRST today.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — thoracic + hip opener. Scorpion stretch new. Primes bent press and flow work.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 12 min — bicep/elbow prep added since preacher curl is early today.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — same flow as Tuesday. Light and easy, legs are tomorrow.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 15 min — lizard pose new. Plank hip dips replace hollow body. 70/side is the floor today.

tips: SHUFFLE WEEK: same exercises, reordered. Push press first on push day, preacher curl early on pull day.
Push press with fresh shoulders: does 50/side finally go overhead? This is the test.
Preacher curl after pull-ups only: find your real working weight without cable curl fatigue.
Thursday KB is LIGHT — 20 min skill work only. No swings, no C&P, no squats. Legs are tomorrow.
Thursday/Friday are swappable — if meetings run late, do legs Thu and KB Fri.
Squat at 70/side for all working sets. Try 75 on the last set if it's clean.
Calf raises: 7 plates. Yes, really.

weeks.label: Lock In
weeks.description: Shuffle order is permanent — push press stays first, preacher stays early. W5 proved the reorder works: 50/side x8 overhead, 15/side preacher honest. Now lock in and progress. Chest press moves to station 2 (fresher). Squat 75/side attempt. DB row 80lb PR. Calves trying 7 plates. KB: full Explore/Refine split, Thursday stays light.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 12 min — band external rotations added for shoulder health. Overhead mobility priority.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — thoracic + hips. Same as W5 but deeper.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 12 min — bicep/elbow prep. Dead hang 25s, scap pull-ups x10.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — same flow as Tuesday. Be boring.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 15 min — 75/side attempt today. Hip mobility matters more than ever.

tips: LOCK IN: shuffle order is permanent. Push press first, preacher early, chest press station 2.
Push press 50/side: 3x8 clean is the target. W5 was 8/8/7.
Chest press at station 2 should be ~160-170 (was 175 first, 140 last).
Pull-ups: CLEAN SETS ONLY. No more fragmenting. 30 clean > 34 messy.
DB row 80lb top set — your back is ready.
Squat 75/side on the last set. Even 5 reps is a win.
Calf raises: 7 plates. Single-leg if you max the machine.
Thursday KB: boring. Legs tomorrow. You know the drill.
Gym days (Push/Pull/Legs) are for building size — go heavy, rest 2-3 min between sets
KB days are for conditioning and fun — lighter weight, keep moving, build work capacity
The 12-min run to/from the full gym = 72 min/week of Zone 2 for free
Day 6 is truly optional — rest if you're beat, do cardio if you're fired up
For the Functional Bulk goal: eat in a slight surplus, prioritize protein, sleep 7-8 hours
Each week has fixed exercises — no randomization — so you can track and beat your numbers
New weeks will be added over time with different exercises, rep schemes, and progressions
restGuidelines: Gym days: 2-3 min between compound sets, 60-90s between isolation. KB days: minimal rest within circuits, 45-60s between rounds. Zone 2: keep HR 120-140bpm.

overview.duration: Multi-week (ongoing — new weeks added over time)
overview.goal: Get functionally large — build size and strength in the gym while maintaining conditioning and athleticism. Unlike the standard version, this tracks your exact exercises week-to-week so you can measure progress.
overview.whoIsItFor: Intermediate lifters who want structure AND progression. You want to know exactly what you're doing each day, track your weights, and see yourself getting stronger week to week.
overview.whatToExpect: 3 heavy gym days (Push/Pull/Legs) for building size, 2 kettlebell days for conditioning, plus an optional Zone 2 day. Each week has a specific focus and fixed exercises. Week 1 establishes your baseline; future weeks introduce new exercises, higher volume, or different rep schemes.
keyPrinciples: Fixed exercises each week — no randomization — so you can track progression
Gym days are for building size — go heavy, rest 2-3 min between sets
KB days are for conditioning — lighter weight, keep moving
Each week has a different focus that builds on the previous
Eat in a slight surplus, prioritize protein, sleep 7-8 hours
Do 10 min daily mobility every morning — it won't affect recovery
overview.cycleNote: This is a multi-week program. Each week has its own set of workouts. Complete all sessions in a week before moving to the next. New weeks are developed over time based on your progress and goals.
overview.weeklyStructure: Mon: Push (Gym) → Tue: KB Full Body → Wed: Pull (Gym) → Thu: KB Conditioning → Fri: Legs (Gym) → Sat: Zone 2/Optional → Sun: Rest
